name: Substly SaaS Subscription Management API
what_it_does: SaaS subscription management platform with API — helping SMBs and mid-market companies track software subscriptions, manage renewals, monitor costs, control access, and avoid shadow IT through a simple dashboard with employee access management and renewal calendar.
use_cases: Tracking all SaaS subscriptions and costs in a centralized dashboard via Substly
Querying subscription renewal dates and costs for IT budget planning and alerts
Managing employee software access and license assignments via Substly
Integrating Substly subscription data with accounting systems for cost allocation
Monitoring SaaS spend trends and identifying unused or redundant subscriptions
not_for: Enterprise SaaS procurement with negotiation (use Vendr or Sastrify for negotiation)
Deep usage analytics at feature level (use Productiv for granular usage data)
Virtual card spend control (use Cledara for card-based SaaS management)
best_when: A small to mid-size company (10-200 employees) needs a simple, affordable way to get visibility into SaaS subscriptions, renewals, and costs without the complexity of enterprise SaaS management platforms.
avoid_when: You're a large enterprise needing procurement negotiation, deep usage analytics, shadow IT discovery, or automated offboarding across many apps.

auth.notes: API key from Substly account settings. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped access. Webhook for renewal alerts and subscription events. Accounting integrations via native connectors. Google SSO and Microsoft SSO for platform access. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.notes: Swedish company (Stockholm). Founded 2019. Bootstrapped. Nordic startup with focus on SMB simplicity. Simple pricing vs enterprise alternatives. Substly positions as affordable alternative to Torii, Zylo for growing companies. Renewal calendar and owner tracking core features. Used by 500+ companies primarily in Nordics and Europe.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for subscriptions, costs, users, and renewals. API key bearer auth. Limited public API documentation — primarily dashboard-driven. Simple data model vs enterprise SaaS management platforms. Webhook for renewal events. Nordic SMB focus — simpler than enterprise alternatives.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscription reads fully idempotent. Cost reads idempotent. User reads idempotent. Renewal reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Limited API documentation — primarily a dashboard product with basic API
Free tier limited to 5 subscriptions — insufficient for visibility into real SaaS portfolios
Nordic/European focus — less US accounting system integration than US competitors
Bootstrapped — smaller team, slower feature velocity than VC-backed competitors
Manual subscription entry vs automatic discovery used by enterprise platforms

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. EU data residency. API key auth. Swedish company with GDPR-first design. Subscription cost data is commercially sensitive — restrict API access. Simple access model without fine-grained scopes.
